chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-1561 of 2024 C. P. No. D-1670 of 2024 C. P. No. D-2303 of 2024 Date Order with signature of Judge For orders as to maintainability of Petition. 19.09.2024. Mr. Ahmed Khan Balouch, Advocate for the Petitioners. ----- The Petitioners have invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution, essentially seeking to recover certain contractual dues, and it being said that the Petitions would lie as there is no denial of liability on the part of the concerned Respondents. However, as it transpires, the matter is covered by a judgment of a learned Division Bench of this Court in the case reported as M/s. Muhammad Usman Lothio vs. Government of Sindh & others SBLR 2023 Sindh 1001, where it was held that a petition for recovery of a contractual claim was not maintainable. Under such circumstances, applying the same principle, the Petitions stand dismissed, leaving the Petitioners at liberty to avail their remedy through a civil proceeding before the competent Court, if so desired. JUDGE JUDGE MUBASHIR
